title: Correctness, Completeness, Optimality, Metrics, and Selection · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved maze trace contains one plausible coordinate, wall, graph, search, event-loop, focus, render, persistence, package, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in solver benchmark and decision report and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate endpoints, adjacency, passability, uniqueness policy, and reported cost against an independent route checker.
State the finite graph and resource assumptions under which a solver will find a path if one exists.
Tie shortest or least-cost claims to edge weights, heuristic properties, termination rule, and verified route cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Correctness Witness
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug puzzle team handles case 2508 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare solver correctness, completeness, optimality, work, memory, and selection on identical fixtures; the prior good state must survive failure; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate endpoints, adjacency, passability, uniqueness policy, and reported cost against an independent route checker.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Correctness Witness, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Correctness Witness record says solver benchmark and decision report looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Correctness Witness.

For inspect the failure record, solve one convenient seeded maze, omit revision, coordinate and wall invariants, topology oracle, frontier trace, changed algorithm or heuristic, callback ownership, keyboard and structured-text access, native Tk evidence, failure, recovery, and package transfer, then accept a screenshot as maze proof. This does not satisfy Correctness Witness for solver benchmark and decision report.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Solver Selection
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-solver-selection-12h99j" that uses case 6250 to compare solver correctness, completeness, optimality, work, memory, and selection on identical fixtures. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Solver Selection.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Solver Selection into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Solver Selection, keep Solver Selection connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-solver-selection-12h99j
# Competency: maze-solver-selection.
# Case 6250: a museum kiosk accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: event-loop work must remain cancellable.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def evidence_maze_solver_selection_12h99j(fixtures: int = 24, valid_results: int = 24, solvers: int = 4, changed_cases: int = 8, isolated_metrics: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_12h99j = "12h99j-j99h21-6250"
    assert evidence_variant_12h99j
    evidence_axes_12h99j = (
        "grid12h99j",
        "topology6250",
        "searchj99h21",
        "event12h99j6250",
        "access625012h99j",
        "recoveryj99h216250",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_12h99j)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or valid_results < 0:
        return False, valid_results, "benchmark-fixtures"
    if valid_results != fixtures:
        return False, valid_results, "invalid-solver-result"
    if solvers < 4 or changed_cases < 4:
        return False, solvers, "solver-coverage"
    if not isolated_metrics:
        return False, changed_cases, "confounded-metrics"
    assert valid_results == fixtures
    return True, valid_results, "defensible-solver-comparison"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
